title: SP031: Variable used in the result set outside aggregate and not mentioned in GROUP BY clause
description:
https://data-interop.era.europa.eu/endpoint
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX era: <http://data.europa.eu/949/>

SELECT ?x (count(*) as ?countries) {
  ?x a era:OperationalPoint; era:inCountry ?y
} group by ?x having (?countries>1)
Causes Virtuoso 37000 Error SP031: SPARQL compiler: Variable ?countries is used in the result set outside aggregate and not mentioned in GROUP BY clause
Jena (https://sparql.org/sparql.html) and Rdf4j (GraphDB) don't complain about this.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE OperationalPoint (id INT PRIMARY KEY, country VARCHAR(100), value INT);
INSERT INTO OperationalPoint (id, country, value) VALUES (1, 'CountryA', 10), (2, 'CountryB', 20), (3, 'CountryA', 15);
CREATE TABLE CountryStats (country VARCHAR(100), total INT);
INSERT INTO CountryStats (country, total) SELECT country, COUNT(*) FROM OperationalPoint GROUP BY country HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT country, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM OperationalPoint WHERE country = CountryStats.country) AS country_count FROM CountryStats WHERE country_count > 1;
key_bug_features: Variable used outside aggregate not in GROUP BY clause
